headlines.title: At the Enhanced Games, drugs don’t get athletes banned. They could get them rich.
headlines.description: When Shania Collins was first approached about taking performance-enhancing drugs last year, it made her nervous enough to contact two members of the Drug Enforcement Administration — her parents.

headlines.title: Spanish government confirms new case of hantavirus
headlines.description: The Spanish government said it has confirmed a new case of hantavirus connected to the cruise ship MV Hondius, which became the center of an outbreak that killed three people earlier this month.

headlines.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
headlines.description: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

headlines.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine
headlines.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the country’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: How violence, misinformation and years of war are impacting the Ebola outbreak in the DRC
headlines.description: As Ebola spreads in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, health officials say violence against health centers and misinformation is making it harder to fight the outbreak. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.

headlines.title: Thousands evacuated from chemical tank threat
headlines.description: In California, 16,000 people remain displaced as officials say a damaged chemical tank is still a threat. In Washington, a deadly chemical disaster at a packaging plant killed multiple people and left others severely injured. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

headlines.title: Why is Dating on the Decline Among Young Adults?
headlines.description: Research shows that Gen Z teens and young adults are dating far less than generations before, so staff reporter for The Atlantic Faith Hill and clinical psychologist Alexandra Solomon join TODAY to break down the “situationship” trend that’s replacing dating. They explain how a society-wide decrease in stigma around singlehood has resulted in less pressure for young people to go on dates, and talk about the fear of rejection that causes Gen Z to shy away from emotional vulnerability. Then,

headlines.title: How to Build Healthy Habits to Improve Longevity and Healthspan
headlines.description: The hot topic of longevity is taking over social media so NBC News medical contributor, Dr. Natalie Azar, and TODAY nutrition expert, Joy Bauer, join TODAY to break down the science behind longevity and share tips for how you can improve your healthy years. They highlight the most important factors to increase your health span including sleep, diet, and exercise, and share small ways you can boost longevity like walking for 10 minutes after meals.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.
